NP Complete Problems

todo PT 3D TS CS NN

Lemma. any 3CNF φ\varphiφ can be converted to an equiv 3CNF φ′\varphi'φ′ such that

φ′\varphi'φ′ uses a superset of φ\varphiφ’s variables φ′\varphi'φ′ has distinct variables in each clause for each satisfying τ\tauτ of φ\varphiφ

  • ∃!τ′\exists!\tau'∃!τ′ that satisfies φ′\varphi'φ′
  • τ′(x)=τ(x)\tau'(x)=\tau(x)τ′(x)=τ(x) for any φ\varphiφ’s variable xxx
Proof. let 3CNF φ\varphiφ

if φ\varphiφ has unique variables in each clause, φ′=φ\varphi'=\varphiφ′=φ done

otherwise, construct φ′\varphi'φ′ as follows

  1. start with a copy of φ\varphiφ
  2. add a,b,ca,b,ca,b,c as new variables
  3. add this to φ′\varphi'φ′, thus to satisfy, we need τ′(a)=τ′(b)=τ′(c)=F\tau'(a)=\tau'(b)=\tau'(c)=Fτ′(a)=τ′(b)=τ′(c)=F
(a‾∨b‾∨c‾)∧(a‾∨b‾∨c)∧(a‾∨b∨c)∧(a∨b‾∨c‾)∨(a∨b‾∨c)∧(a∨b∨c‾)\small(\overline a\lor\overline b\lor\overline c)\land(\overline a\lor\overline b \lor c)\land(\overline a\lor b\lor c)\land(a\lor\overline b\lor\overline c)\lor(a\lor\overline b\lor c)\land(a\lor b\lor \overline c)(a∨b∨c)∧(a∨b∨c)∧(a∨b∨c)∧(a∨b∨c)∨(a∨b∨c)∧(a∨b∨c)
  1. replace each (x∨x‾∨y)(x\lor\overline x\lor y)(x∨x∨y) with (x∨a‾∨y)(x\lor \overline a\lor y)(x∨a∨y)
  2. replace each (x∨x∨y)(x\lor x\lor y)(x∨x∨y) with (x∨a∨y)(x\lor a\lor y)(x∨a∨y)
  3. replace each (x∨x∨x)(x\lor x\lor x)(x∨x∨x) with (x∨a∨b)(x\lor a\lor b)(x∨a∨b)

therefore

  • φ′\varphi'φ′ uses same vars as φ\varphiφ, with additional a,b,ca,b,ca,b,c
  • each satisfying τ\tauτ corresponds to τ′\tau'τ′ with the same assigments and a,b,ca,b,ca,b,c to FFF
  • φ′\varphi'φ′ has unique variables in each clause □\square□

and construction is polytime

X1-3SAT Instance: 3CNF φ\varphiφ
Question: is there satisfying τ\tauτ where only one literal is true in each clause

Proof. 3S⩽pX13S\le_p X13S⩽p​X1 let φ\varphiφ be an instance of 3S3S3S, assume has unique variables
construct φ′\varphi'φ′ as conjuction of the following 3CNF C′C'C′s

  • for each clause C=(ℓ1∨ℓ2∨ℓ3)C=(\ell_1\lor \ell_2\lor \ell_3)C=(ℓ1​∨ℓ2​∨ℓ3​) in φ\varphiφ
    • let a1,a2,a3,b1,b2,b3a_1,a_2,a_3,b_1,b_2,b_3a1​,a2​,a3​,b1​,b2​,b3​ be new variables
    • construct C′C'C′ that corresponds with CCC
C′=(a1∨a2∨a3)∧(a1∨ℓ‾1∨b1)∧(a2∨ℓ‾2∨b2)∧(a3∨ℓ‾3∨b3)C'=(a_1\lor a_2\lor a_3)\land(a_1\lor\overline\ell_1\lor b_1)\land(a_2\lor\overline\ell_2\lor b_2)\land(a_3\lor\overline\ell_3\lor b_3)C′=(a1​∨a2​∨a3​)∧(a1​∨ℓ1​∨b1​)∧(a2​∨ℓ2​∨b2​)∧(a3​∨ℓ3​∨b3​)

(  ⟹  )(\implies)(⟹) assume τ(φ)=T\tau(\varphi)=Tτ(φ)=T

  • let τ′\tau'τ′ be a truth assignment of φ′\varphi'φ′, defined later
  • consider each satisfied clause CCC in φ\varphiφ
    • let ℓi\ell_iℓi​ be an true literal
    • let τ(ai)=T\tau(a_i)=Tτ(ai​)=T and τ(bi)=F\tau(b_i)=Fτ(bi​)=F, thus (ai∨ℓ‾i∨bi)(a_i\lor\overline\ell_i\lor b_i)(ai​∨ℓi​∨bi​) is X1X1X1
    • for each j≠ij\ne ij=i
      • let τ(aj)=F\tau(a_j)=Fτ(aj​)=F, thus (a1∨a2∨a3)(a_1\lor a_2\lor a_3)(a1​∨a2​∨a3​) is X1X1X1
      • let τ(b)=τ(ℓ1)\tau(b)=\tau(\ell_1)τ(b)=τ(ℓ1​), thus (aj∨ℓ‾j∨bj)(a_j\lor\overline\ell_j\lor b_j)(aj​∨ℓj​∨bj​) is X1X1X1
  • thus ⟨φ′⟩∈X1\langle \varphi'\rangle\in X1⟨φ′⟩∈X1, with τ′\tau'τ′ as cert

(  ⟸  )(\impliedby)(⟸) assume τ′(φ′)=T\tau'(\varphi')=Tτ′(φ′)=T

  • let φ\varphiφ be a TA for φ\varphiφ, defined as φ′\varphi'φ′ without assignment to aia_iai​ and bib_ibi​
  • for each satisfied clause C′C'C′ in φ′\varphi'φ′
    • let aia_iai​ where φ′(ai)=T\varphi'(a_i)=Tφ′(ai​)=T
    • ∴τ(ℓ‾i)=F\therefore\tau(\overline\ell_i)=F∴τ(ℓi​)=F, i.e. τ(ℓi)=T\tau(\ell_i)=Tτ(ℓi​)=T as per (ai∨ℓ‾i∨bi)(a_i\lor\overline\ell_i\lor b_i)(ai​∨ℓi​∨bi​) in C′C'C′
    • ∴τ(ℓi)=T\therefore\tau(\ell_i)=T∴τ(ℓi​)=T in the corresponding CCC in φ\varphiφ
  • ∴τ(φ)=T\therefore\tau(\varphi)=T∴τ(φ)=T, thus ⟨φ⟩∈3S\langle \varphi\rangle\in 3S⟨φ⟩∈3S

let nnn be number of clauses

  • φ′\varphi'φ′ has 4n4n4n clauses, 3n3n3n more variables
  • ∴\therefore∴ polytime □\square□

NAE-3SAT (Not-All-Equal 3SAT) Instance: 3CNF φ\varphiφ
Question: is there satisfying τ\tauτ where each clause has a TTT literal and a FFF literal

Proof. 3S⩽pN33S\le_pN33S⩽p​N3 let φ\varphiφ be an instance of 3S3S3S, construct φ′\varphi'φ′ for N3N3N3 as follows:

  • ttt is a new variable in φ′\varphi'φ′
  • φ′\varphi'φ′ is conjuction of the 3CNF C′C'C′s where
  • for each clause C=(ℓ1∨ℓ2∨ℓ3)C=(\ell_1\lor\ell_2\lor\ell_3)C=(ℓ1​∨ℓ2​∨ℓ3​) in φ\varphiφ
    • let a1,a2,a3a_1,a_2,a_3a1​,a2​,a3​ be new variables
    • construct C′C'C′ to correspond with CCC
C′=(t‾∨ℓ1∨a1)∧(a‾1∨ℓ2∨a2)∧(a‾2∨ℓ3∨a3)∧(a3∨t∨t)C'=(\overline t\lor\ell_1\lor a_1)\land(\overline a_1\lor\ell_2\lor a_2)\land(\overline a_2\lor\ell_3\lor a_3)\land(a_3\lor t\lor t)C′=(t∨ℓ1​∨a1​)∧(a1​∨ℓ2​∨a2​)∧(a2​∨ℓ3​∨a3​)∧(a3​∨t∨t)

(  ⟹  )(\implies)(⟹) let τ\tauτ that satisfies φ\varphiφ

  • let τ′\tau'τ′ be truth assignment for φ′\varphi'φ′
    • τ′(x)=τ(x)\tau'(x)=\tau(x)τ′(x)=τ(x) and τ′(t)=T\tau'(t)=Tτ′(t)=T
  • for each satisfied clause CCC in φ\varphiφ
    • let τ′(a1)=τ′(ℓ1)‾\tau'(a_1)=\overline{\tau'(\ell_1)}τ′(a1​)=τ′(ℓ1​)​
    • let τ′(a2)=τ′(ℓ2)‾∧τ′(a1)\tau'(a_2)=\overline{\tau'(\ell_2)}\land\tau'(a_1)τ′(a2​)=τ′(ℓ2​)​∧τ′(a1​)
    • let τ′(a3)=τ′(ℓ3)‾∧τ′(a2)\tau'(a_3)=\overline{\tau'(\ell_3)}\land\tau'(a_2)τ′(a3​)=τ′(ℓ3​)​∧τ′(a2​)
    • ∴τ′(ℓ1)=T\therefore\tau'(\ell_1)=T∴τ′(ℓ1​)=T implies τ′\tau'τ′ assigns C′C'C′ to
      (F∨T∨F)∧(T∨ℓ2∨F)∧(T∨ℓ3∨F)∧(F∨T∨T)(F\lor T\lor F)\land(T\lor\ell_2\lor F)\land(T\lor\ell_3\lor F)\land(F\lor T\lor T)(F∨T∨F)∧(T∨ℓ2​∨F)∧(T∨ℓ3​∨F)∧(F∨T∨T)
      τ′(ℓ1)=F\tau'(\ell_1)=Fτ′(ℓ1​)=F and τ′(ℓ2)=T\tau'(\ell_2)=Tτ′(ℓ2​)=T implies τ′\tau'τ′ assigns C′C'C′ to
      (F∨F∨T)∧(F∨T∨F)∨(T∨ℓ3∨F)∧(F∨T∨T)(F\lor F\lor T)\land(F\lor T\lor F)\lor(T\lor\ell_3\lor F)\land(F\lor T\lor T)(F∨F∨T)∧(F∨T∨F)∨(T∨ℓ3​∨F)∧(F∨T∨T)
      τ′(ℓ1)=τ′(ℓ2)=F\tau'(\ell_1)=\tau'(\ell_2)=Fτ′(ℓ1​)=τ′(ℓ2​)=F and τ′(ℓ3)=T\tau'(\ell_3)=Tτ′(ℓ3​)=T implies τ′\tau'τ′ assigns C′C'C′ to
      (F∨F∨T)∧(F∨F∨T)∧(F∨T∨F)∧(F∨T∨T)(F\lor F\lor T)\land(F\lor F\lor T)\land(F\lor T\lor F)\land(F\lor T\lor T)(F∨F∨T)∧(F∨F∨T)∧(F∨T∨F)∧(F∨T∨T)
    • ∴\therefore∴ in any case, C′C'C′ is NAE
  • ∴⟨φ′⟩∈N3\therefore\langle \varphi'\rangle\in N3∴⟨φ′⟩∈N3 with φ′\varphi'φ′ as cert

(  ⟸  )(\impliedby)(⟸) let τ′\tau'τ′ satisfy φ′\varphi'φ′

  • ∴τ′(φ′)=T=τ′‾(φ′)\therefore\tau'(\varphi')=T=\overline{\tau'}(\varphi')∴τ′(φ′)=T=τ′(φ′) since τ′\tau'τ′ is NAE
  • ∴\therefore∴ WLOG assume τ′(t)=T\tau'(t)=Tτ′(t)=T, let τ\tauτ be TA for φ\varphiφ where τ(x)=τ′(x)\tau(x)=\tau'(x)τ(x)=τ′(x) for any xxx
  • BWOC assume ∃C\exists C∃C in φ\varphiφ such that τ(ℓi)=F\tau(\ell_i)=Fτ(ℓi​)=F for any literal ℓ\ellℓ
    • ∴\therefore∴ to ensure NAE, the corresponding C′C'C′ evaluates to
      (F∨F∨T)∧(F∨F∨T)∧(F∨F∨a3)∧(a3∨T∨T)(F\lor F\lor T)\land(F\lor F\lor T)\land(F\lor F\lor a_3)\land(a_3\lor T\lor T)(F∨F∨T)∧(F∨F∨T)∧(F∨F∨a3​)∧(a3​∨T∨T)
    • ∴τ′\therefore\tau'∴τ′ can’t assign a3a_3a3​ to make NAE
  • ∴τ(φ)=T\therefore\tau(\varphi)=T∴τ(φ)=T, thus ⟨φ⟩∈3S\langle \varphi\rangle\in 3S⟨φ⟩∈3S

let nnn be number of clauses in φ\varphiφ

  • ϕ′\phi'ϕ′ has 4n4n4n clauses, thus polytime □\square□

CLIQUE Instance: ⟨G,k⟩\langle G,k\rangle⟨G,k⟩ for graph GGG and k∈Nk\in\mathbb Nk∈N

Question: does GGG have a clique of size kkk

Definition. clique

for graph GGG, set C⊆V(G)C\sube V(G)C⊆V(G) is a clique iff

∀u,v∈V(G)u,v∈C  ⟹  {u,v}∈E(G)\forall u,v\in V(G)\enspace u,v\in C\implies \{u,v\}\in E(G)∀u,v∈V(G)u,v∈C⟹{u,v}∈E(G)
Proof. CL∈NPCL\in NPCL∈NP

is decision

cert exists

  • for ⟨G,k⟩∈CL\langle G,k\rangle\in CL⟨G,k⟩∈CL, let cert C⊆V(G)C\sube V(G)C⊆V(G) be a clique of size at least kkk in graph GGG

cert polysize

  • since C⊆V(G)C\sube V(G)C⊆V(G), we have ∣C∣⩽∣V(G)∣|C|\le |V(G)|∣C∣⩽∣V(G)∣, thus polysize wrt ⟨G⟩\langle G\rangle⟨G⟩

verifier

  • let VVV be on input ⟨G,k,c⟩\langle G,k,c\rangle⟨G,k,c⟩
    • let c={v1,v2,⋯ ,vn}c=\{v_1,v_2,\cdots,v_n\}c={v1​,v2​,⋯,vn​}
    • check n⩾kn\ge kn⩾k
    • for any (u,v)∈c×c(u,v)\in c\times c(u,v)∈c×c where u≠vu\ne vu=v
      • check GGG has edge (u,v)(u,v)(u,v)
    • if all check passes { acc } else { rej }

verifies polytime

  • each “check” is O(1)\mathcal O(1)O(1)
  • the loop at most ∣C∣2|C|^2∣C∣2 iters, which is at most ∣G(V)∣2|G(V)|^2∣G(V)∣2, thus polytime

Proof. 3S⩽pCL3S\le_p CL3S⩽p​CL let FFF be on input ⟨φ⟩\langle \varphi\rangle⟨φ⟩

  • let kkk be number of clauses in φ\varphiφ
  • make graph GGG
  • for each clause, make 3 nodes in GGG
  • for the edges in GGG
    • no edge between nodes in the same clause
    • no edge between literals xix_ixi​ and xi‾\overline{x_i}xi​​
    • add edge otherwise
  • return ⟨G,k⟩\langle G,k\rangle⟨G,k⟩
e.g. φ=(x1‾∨x2‾∨x3)∧(x1∨x2∨x3‾)∧(x1‾∨x2‾∨x3‾)∧(x1∨x2‾∨x3‾)\varphi=(\overline{x_1} \lor \overline{x_2} \lor x_3)\land(x_1 \lor x_2 \lor \overline{x_3})\land(\overline{x_1} \lor \overline{x_2} \lor \overline{x_3})\land(x_1 \lor \overline{x_2} \lor \overline{x_3})φ=(x1​​∨x2​​∨x3​)∧(x1​∨x2​∨x3​​)∧(x1​​∨x2​​∨x3​​)∧(x1​∨x2​​∨x3​​)
wts polytime, let nnn be number of clauses in φ\varphiφ
  • GGG has 3k3k3k nodes, at most 9k/29k/29k/2 edges
  • ∴O(n)\therefore\mathcal O(n)∴O(n) times, thus polytime

wts ⟨φ⟩∈3S  ⟺  ⟨G,k⟩∈CL\langle \varphi\rangle\in 3S\iff\langle G,k\rangle\in CL⟨φ⟩∈3S⟺⟨G,k⟩∈CL

  • (==>) assume ⟨φ⟩∈3S\langle \varphi\rangle\in 3S⟨φ⟩∈3S
    • ∴∃τ\therefore\exists \tau∴∃τ that satisfies φ\varphiφ
    • ∴\therefore∴ let C⊆V(G)C\sube V(G)C⊆V(G) be made by picking one true literal in each triple/clause
    • ∴∣C∣=k\therefore |C|=k∴∣C∣=k and CCC is clique, thus ⟨G,k⟩∈CL\langle G,k\rangle\in CL⟨G,k⟩∈CL
  • (<==) assume ⟨G,k⟩∈CL\langle G,k\rangle\in CL⟨G,k⟩∈CL
    • ∴∃C⊆V(G)\therefore\exists C\sube V(G)∴∃C⊆V(G) as a kkk-clique
    • ∵\because∵ no edge between nodes in the same triple/clause
    • ∴C\therefore C∴C contains only 1 node in each of kkk clause
    • ∴\therefore∴ let τ\tauτ assign the variables such that the literals in CCC are true
    • ∴τ\therefore\tau∴τ satisfies φ\varphiφ, thus ⟨φ⟩∈3S\langle \varphi\rangle\in 3S⟨φ⟩∈3S

INDEPENDENT-SET Instance: graph GGG and k∈Nk\in\mathbb Nk∈N
Question: does GGG has an indepdent set of size kkk

Definition. indepdent set III of size kkk
  • I⊆V(G)I\sube V(G)I⊆V(G)
  • ∣I∣=k|I|=k∣I∣=k
  • ∀u≠v∈I{u,v}∉E\forall u\ne v\in I\enspace \{u,v\}\not\in E∀u=v∈I{u,v}∈E

Proof. CL⩽pISCL\le_p ISCL⩽p​IS let ⟨G,k⟩\langle G,k\rangle⟨G,k⟩ be instance of CLCLCL, construct ⟨G‾,k⟩\langle \overline G,k\rangle⟨G,k⟩ as instance of ISISIS, where

  • G‾=(V,E‾)\overline G=(V,\overline E)G=(V,E)
  • E‾=(V×V)∖E\overline E=(V\times V)\setminus EE=(V×V)∖E

(  ⟺  )(\iff)(⟺) GGG has a kkk clique C⊆V(G)C\sube V(G)C⊆V(G)

  •   ⟺  ∀u≠v∈C{u,v}∈E\iff\forall u\ne v\in C\enspace \{u,v\}\in E⟺∀u=v∈C{u,v}∈E
  •   ⟺  ∀u≠v∈C{u,v}∉E‾\iff\forall u\ne v\in C\enspace\{u,v\}\not\in \overline E⟺∀u=v∈C{u,v}∈E
  •   ⟺  C\iff C⟺C is a kkk IS in G‾\overline GG

polytime, of course □\square□

VERTEX-COVER Instance: graph GGG and k∈Nk\in\mathbb Nk∈N
Question: does GGG have a vertex cover of size kkk

Definition. vertex cover V′⊆VV'\sube VV′⊆V of size kkk
  • ∣V′∣=k|V'|=k∣V′∣=k
  • ∀{u,v}∈Eu∈V′\forall\{u,v\}\in E\enspace u\in V'∀{u,v}∈Eu∈V′ or v∈V′v\in V'v∈V′

Proof. IS⩽pVCIS\le_p VCIS⩽p​VC let ⟨G,k⟩\langle G,k\rangle⟨G,k⟩ be an instance of ISISIS, construct ⟨G,n−k⟩\langle G,n-k\rangle⟨G,n−k⟩ as an instance of VCVCVC, where

  • n=∣V(G)∣n=|V(G)|n=∣V(G)∣

GGG has a size kkk IS III

  •   ⟺  ∀u≠vu∈I and v∈I  ⟹  {u,v}∉E\iff\forall u\ne v\quad u\in I\textsf{ and }v\in I\implies\{u,v\}\not\in E⟺∀u=vu∈I and v∈I⟹{u,v}∈E
  •   ⟺  ∀u≠v{u,v}∈E  ⟹  u∉I or v∉I\iff\forall u\ne v\quad\{u,v\}\in E\implies u\not\in I\textsf{ or }v\not\in I⟺∀u=v{u,v}∈E⟹u∈I or v∈I
  •   ⟺  ∀u≠v{u,v}∈E  ⟹  u∈V∖I\iff\forall u\ne v\quad\{u,v\}\in E\implies u\in V\setminus I⟺∀u=v{u,v}∈E⟹u∈V∖I or v∈V∖Iv\in V\setminus Iv∈V∖I
  •   ⟺  V∖I\iff V\setminus I⟺V∖I is a ∣V∖I∣=n−k|V\setminus I|=n-k∣V∖I∣=n−k size VC of GGG

and in polytime □\square□

3COL (colorability) Instance: graph G=(V,E)G=(V,E)G=(V,E)
Question: does GGG has a 333 coloring

Proof. 3S⩽p3C3S\le_p3C3S⩽p​3C let φ\varphiφ be an instance of 3S3S3S, construct GGG as follows

  • include a K3K_3K3​ as the palette, name the vertices as follows:
  • for each variable xix_ixi​ in φ\varphiφ, make this literal widget
    • dashed node means reuse node from previous step
  • for each clause C=ℓ1∨ℓ2∨ℓ3C=\ell_1\lor\ell_2\lor\ell_3C=ℓ1​∨ℓ2​∨ℓ3​, make this OR gates
    • the literal nodes are reused from the literal widgets

(  ⟹  )(\implies)(⟹) assume τ(φ)=T\tau(\varphi)=Tτ(φ)=T

  • ∴\therefore∴ color the palette by the node names
    • for each clause C=ℓ1∨ℓ2∨ℓ3C=\ell_1\lor\ell_2\lor\ell_3C=ℓ1​∨ℓ2​∨ℓ3​
      • color node ℓi\ell_iℓi​ to τ(ℓi)\tau(\ell_i)τ(ℓi​)
  • ∴\therefore∴ the rest of GGG can be 3 colored
examples
  • if color ℓ1\ell_1ℓ1​ or ℓ2\ell_2ℓ2​ to TTT
  • if color ℓ3\ell_3ℓ3​ to TTT

(  ⟸  )(\impliedby)(⟸) assume GGG is 3 colored

  • WLOG assume palette is colored by its name
  • ∵\because∵ literal nodes are corrected to #\##
  • ∴\therefore∴ literal nodes must be colored TTT or FFF
  • ∵xi\because x_i∵xi​ is corrected to xi‾\overline{x_i}xi​​
  • ∴\therefore∴ the literal nodes represents a valid truth assignment τ\tauτ
  • ∴τ\therefore\tau∴τ satisfies each clause in φ\varphiφ
otherwise…
  • ∴τ(φ)=T\therefore\tau(\varphi)=T∴τ(φ)=T

assume φ\varphiφ has kkk clauses and nnn variables

  • ∴G\therefore G∴G uses at most 3+2n+6k3+2n+6k3+2n+6k nodes, thus polytime □\square□

HAMPATH Instance: ⟨G,s,t⟩\langle G,s,t\rangle⟨G,s,t⟩ for directed graph G=(V,E)G=(V,E)G=(V,E) and s,t∈Vs,t\in Vs,t∈V

Question: is there a hampath from sss to ttt

Definition. hamiltonian path a hampath from sss to ttt is a path that goes through every node only once

Proof. HP∈NPHP\in NPHP∈NP is decision

cert exists

  • for any ⟨G,s,t⟩∈HP\langle G,s,t\rangle\in\text{HP}⟨G,s,t⟩∈HP, let certificate ccc be a hampath of GGG from sss to ttt, represented as a finite sequence (s,v1,v2,⋯ ,t)(s,v_1,v_2,\cdots,t)(s,v1​,v2​,⋯,t)

cert polysize

  • the hampath as a sequence has length exactly ∣V∣|V|∣V∣, thus ⟨c⟩\langle c\rangle⟨c⟩ is polysize wrt the input

verifier

  • let VVV be on input ⟨G,s,t,c⟩\langle G,s,t,c\rangle⟨G,s,t,c⟩
    • let (v0,v1,⋯ ,vn)=c(v_0,v_1,\cdots,v_n)=c(v0​,v1​,⋯,vn​)=c
    • check v0=sv_0=sv0​=s and vn=tv_n=tvn​=t
    • for iii in 000 to n−1n-1n−1
      • check (vi,vi+1)(v_i,v_i+1)(vi​,vi​+1) is an edge in GGG
    • check every vertex in GGG occurs only once in ccc
    • if all checks pass { acc } else { rej }

verifier polytime

  • each check is O(1)\mathcal O(1)O(1) and the for loop iters ∣V∣|V|∣V∣ times
  • ∴ VVV runs in polytime
Proof. 3S⩽pHP3S\le_p HP3S⩽p​HP

let FFF be on input ⟨φ⟩\langle \varphi\rangle⟨φ⟩

  • let c1,⋯ ,ckc_1,\cdots,c_kc1​,⋯,ck​ be clauses and x1,⋯ ,xnx_1,\cdots,x_nx1​,⋯,xn​ be variables

  • make graph GGG as follows:

    • for each clause cic_ici​ make a node
    • for each variable xix_ixi​ make this subgraph:

  • connect the xix_ixi​ subgraphs as follows:

  • for any xix_ixi​ and cjc_jcj​
    • if xix_ixi​ in cjc_jcj​, add edges cij→cj→cij′\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{ij}}\to \htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}\to \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}cij​→cj​→cij′​
    • if xi‾\overline{x_i}xi​​ in cjc_jcj​, add edges cij′→cj→cij\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}\to \htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}\to \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{ij}}cij′​→cj​→cij​
  • return ⟨G,v0,vn⟩\langle G,v_0,v_n\rangle⟨G,v0​,vn​⟩

wts ⟨φ⟩∈3S  ⟺  ⟨G,v0,vn⟩∈HP\langle \varphi\rangle\in 3S\iff\langle G,v_0,v_n\rangle\in HP⟨φ⟩∈3S⟺⟨G,v0​,vn​⟩∈HP (==>) assume ⟨φ⟩∈3S\langle \varphi\rangle\in 3S⟨φ⟩∈3S, i.e. ∃τ\exists\tau∃τ that satisfies φ\varphiφ,

  • wts GGG has hampath v0⇝vnv_0\leadsto v_nv0​⇝vn​
  • starting at v0v_0v0​, on each subgraph xix_ixi​
    • if τ(xi)=T\tau(x_i)=Tτ(xi​)=T, then go vi−1→xi⇝xi‾→viv_{i-1}\to \htmlClass{text-teal}{x_i}\leadsto\htmlClass{text-teal}{\overline{x_i}}\to v_ivi−1​→xi​⇝xi​​→vi​
    • if τ(xi)=F\tau(x_i)=Fτ(xi​)=F, then go vi−1→xi‾⇝xi→viv_{i-1}\to\htmlClass{text-teal}{\overline{x_i}}\leadsto \htmlClass{text-teal}{x_i}\to v_ivi−1​→xi​​⇝xi​→vi​
  • to cover each cj\htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}cj​ node
    • if xix_ixi​ in cjc_jcj​ is TTT, then go xi⇝cij→cj→cij′⇝xi‾\htmlClass{text-teal}{x_i}\leadsto \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{ij}}\to \htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}\to \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}\leadsto\htmlClass{text-teal}{\overline{x_i}}xi​⇝cij​→cj​→cij′​⇝xi​​
    • if xi‾\overline{x_i}xi​​ in cjc_jcj​ is TTT, then go xi‾⇝cij′→cj→cij⇝xi\htmlClass{text-teal}{\overline{x_i}}\leadsto \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}\to \htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}\to \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{ij}}\leadsto \htmlClass{text-teal}{x_i}xi​​⇝cij′​→cj​→cij​⇝xi​
  • ∴\therefore∴ has hampath, thus ⟨G,v0,vn⟩∈HP\langle G,v_0,v_n\rangle\in HP⟨G,v0​,vn​⟩∈HP

(<==) assume ⟨G,v0,vn⟩∈HP\langle G,v_0,v_n\rangle\in HP⟨G,v0​,vn​⟩∈HP, i.e. ∃\exists∃ hampath v0⇝pvnv_0\stackrel{p}{\leadsto} v_nv0​⇝p​vn​

  • wts ∃τ\exists\tau∃τ satisfies φ\varphiφ
  • wts ∀j\forall j∀j, if ppp covers cj\htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}cj​, then ppp must go (for some iii)
    • cij→cj→cij′\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{ij}}\to \htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}\to \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}cij​→cj​→cij′​ or cij′→cj→cij\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}\to \htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}\to \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{ij}}cij′​→cj​→cij​
    • BWOC assume no, WLOG assume caj→cj→cbj\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{aj}}\to\htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}\to\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{bj}}caj​→cj​→cbj​ for a≠ba\ne ba=b
    • then to cover cij′\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}cij′​, ppp must go uij→cij′u_{ij}\to\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}uij​→cij′​ because cij\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{ij}}cij​ and ci\htmlClass{text-sky}{c_i}ci​ are covered
    • but going uij→cij′u_{ij}\to\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}uij​→cij′​ leads to deadend, thus not hampath
  • thus path behaves
    • if cij→cj→cij′\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{ij}}\to \htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}\to \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}cij​→cj​→cij′​ (left to right), let τ(xi)=T\tau(x_i)=Tτ(xi​)=T
    • if cij′→cj→cij\htmlClass{text-yellow}{c'_{ij}}\to \htmlClass{text-sky}{c_j}\to \htmlClass{text-yellow}{c_{ij}}cij′​→cj​→cij​ (right to left), let τ(xi)=F\tau(x_i)=Fτ(xi​)=F
  • ∴τ\therefore\tau∴τ satisfies φ\varphiφ, thus ⟨φ⟩∈3S□\langle \varphi\rangle\in 3S\quad\square⟨φ⟩∈3S□

HAM-CYCLE Instance: directed graph GGG
Question: does GGG has a hamiltonian cycle (passes each vertex only once)

Proof. HP⩽pHCHP\le_p HCHP⩽p​HC let ⟨G,v1,vn⟩\langle G,v_1,v_n\rangle⟨G,v1​,vn​⟩ be an instance of HPHPHP, construct ⟨G′⟩\langle G'\rangle⟨G′⟩ as instance of HCHCHC where

  • V′=V(G′)=V(G)∪{u}V'=V(G')=V(G)\cup\{u\}V′=V(G′)=V(G)∪{u} for u∉Vu\not\in Vu∈V (i.e. add a new vertex)
  • E′=E(G′)=E(G)∪{(vn,u),(u,v1)}E'=E(G')=E(G)\cup\{(v_n,u),(u,v_1)\}E′=E(G′)=E(G)∪{(vn​,u),(u,v1​)}

(  ⟹  )(\implies)(⟹) assume GGG has hampath v1⇝vnv_1{\leadsto} v_nv1​⇝vn​

  • ∴u→v1⇝vn→u\therefore u\to v_1\leadsto v_n\to u∴u→v1​⇝vn​→u is a hamcycle in G′G'G′
  • ∴⟨G′⟩∈HC\therefore\langle G'\rangle\in HC∴⟨G′⟩∈HC

(  ⟸  )(\impliedby)(⟸) assume G′G'G′ has hamcycle p:u⇝up:u\leadsto up:u⇝u

  • ∵u→v1\because u\to v_1∵u→v1​ is only out edge of uuu, vn→uv_n\to uvn​→u is only in edge of $$
  • ∴p:u→v1⇝vn→u\therefore p:u\to v_1\leadsto v_n\to u∴p:u→v1​⇝vn​→u
  • ∴\therefore∴ the sub path v1⇝vnv_1\leadsto v_nv1​⇝vn​ is a hampath of GGG, thus G∈HPG\in HPG∈HP

and polytime □\square□

3SAT Instance: ⟨φ⟩\langle \varphi\rangle⟨φ⟩ where boolean formula φ\varphiφ is in 3CNF

Question: is there a truth assignment τ\tauτ that satisfies φ\varphiφ

Definition. 3CNF and TA
  • φ\varphiφ is the conjunction of 3-clauses
    • 3-clause is disjunction of 3 literals
    • literal is xxx or x‾\overline xx (not x) for x∈PVx\in PVx∈PV
  • τ:PV→{T,F}\tau:PV\to\{T,F\}τ:PV→{T,F}
Proof. 3S∈NP3S\in NP3S∈NP

is decision, cert exists and polysize

verifier exists

  • let VVV be on input ⟨φ,τ⟩\langle\varphi,\tau\rangle⟨φ,τ⟩
    • set every variable of φ\varphiφ according to τ\tauτ and evaluate it
    • if evals to TTT { acc } else { rej }

verifies polytime

INDUCED-PATH Instance: graph GGG and k∈Nk\in\mathbb Nk∈N
Question: is there V′⊆VV'\sube VV′⊆V such that ∣V′∣⩾k|V'|\ge k∣V′∣⩾k and the subgraph induced by V′V'V′ is a simple path of ∣V′∣|V'|∣V′∣ vertices

Proof. 3S⩽pIP3S\le_p IP3S⩽p​IP let φ\varphiφ be instance of 3S3S3S with mmm clauses and nnn variables

  • WLOG assume φ\varphiφ has distinct vars in each clause, then construct GGG as follows:
  • add vertices {v0,⋯ ,vn=c0,c1,⋯ ,cm}\{v_0,\cdots, v_n=c_0,c_1,\cdots,c_m\}{v0​,⋯,vn​=c0​,c1​,⋯,cm​}
  • for 0⩽i<n0\le i\lt n0⩽i<n, connect viv_ivi​ to vi+1v_{i+1}vi+1​ with these literal widgets
  • for 0⩽j<m0\le j\lt m0⩽j<m, connect cjc_jcj​ to cj+1c_{j+1}cj+1​ with these clause widgets
    • connect each ℓi\ell_iℓi​ to ℓi‾\overline{\ell_i}ℓi​​ in the literal widget
  • let k=3m+3n+(m+n+1)=4m+4n+1k=3m+3n+(m+n+1)=4m+4n+1k=3m+3n+(m+n+1)=4m+4n+1

(  ⟹  )(\implies)(⟹) assume τ(φ)=T\tau(\varphi)=Tτ(φ)=T

  • let V′V'V′ be as follows:
    • contains vi,ui,ui′,cj,dj,dj′v_i,u_i,u_i',c_j,d_j,d_j'vi​,ui​,ui′​,cj​,dj​,dj′​ for any i,ji,ji,j
    • in literal widgets, for each variable xix_ixi​
      • τ(xi)=T  ⟹  xi∈V′\tau(x_i)=T\implies x_i\in V'τ(xi​)=T⟹xi​∈V′
      • τ(xi)=F  ⟹  xi‾∈V′\tau(x_i)=F\implies \overline{x_i}\in V'τ(xi​)=F⟹xi​​∈V′
    • in clause widgets, for each literals ℓ1,ℓ2,ℓ3\ell_1,\ell_2,\ell_3ℓ1​,ℓ2​,ℓ3​
      • ℓi∈V′\ell_i\in V'ℓi​∈V′ where τ(ℓi)=T\tau(\ell_i)=Tτ(ℓi​)=T (which exists since τ(φ)=T\tau(\varphi)=Tτ(φ)=T)
  • ∴∣V′∣=k\therefore|V'|=k∴∣V′∣=k
  • consider graph G′=(V′,⋯ )G'=(V',\cdots)G′=(V′,⋯) induced by VVV from G′G'G′
  • ∴G′\therefore G'∴G′ is connected and is path v0⇝cmv_0\leadsto c_mv0​⇝cm​
    • ∵xi∈V′\because x_i\in V'∵xi​∈V′ xor xi‾∈V′\overline{x_i}\in V'xi​​∈V′
    • ∴\therefore∴ each literal widget is a simple path
    • ∵ℓi‾∉V′\because\overline{\ell_i}\not\in V'∵ℓi​​∈V′ as τ(ℓi)=T\tau(\ell_i)=Tτ(ℓi​)=T
    • ∴\therefore∴ each clause widget is a simple path

(  ⟸  )(\impliedby)(⟸) assume V′⊆VV'\sube VV′⊆V induces path ppp with k=(3m+3n)+m+n+1k=(3m+3n)+m+n+1k=(3m+3n)+m+n+1 vertices

  • consider each literal widget
    • ∵\because∵ choosing more that 3 vertices for ppp causes a cycle
    • ∴\therefore∴ each literal widget must have at most 3 vertices in ppp
  • consider each clause widget
    • ∵\because∵ choosing more that 3 vertices for ppp causes branches/claws or cycle
    • ∴\therefore∴ each clause widget must have at most 3 vertices in ppp
  • ∴\therefore∴ the literal and clause widgets must contribute at most 3m+3n3m+3n3m+3n vertices in ppp
  • ∴p\therefore p∴p has m+n+1m+n+1m+n+1 vertices not in the widgets, which are exactly {v0,⋯ ,vn=c0,c1,⋯ ,cm}\{v_0,\cdots,v_n=c_0,c_1,\cdots,c_m\}{v0​,⋯,vn​=c0​,c1​,⋯,cm​}
  • ∵v0\because v_0∵v0​ and cmc_mcm​ have degree 1 in GGG, thus they must be the two ends of ppp
  • ∴\therefore∴ WLOG assume ppp is v0⇝cmv_0\leadsto c_mv0​⇝cm​
    • ∴p\therefore p∴p must be v0⇝v1⇝⋯⇝vn⇝c1⇝⋯⇝cmv_0\leadsto v_1\leadsto\cdots\leadsto v_n\leadsto c_1\leadsto\cdots\leadsto c_mv0​⇝v1​⇝⋯⇝vn​⇝c1​⇝⋯⇝cm​
  • ∴\therefore∴ define τ\tauτ as follows: consider each vi⇝vi+1v_i\leadsto v_{i+1}vi​⇝vi+1​ in ppp
    • vi→ui→xi→ui+1′→vi+1  ⟹  v_i\to u_i\to x_i\to u'_{i+1}\to v_{i+1}\impliesvi​→ui​→xi​→ui+1′​→vi+1​⟹ let τ(xi)=T\tau(x_i)=Tτ(xi​)=T
    • vi→ui→xi‾→ui+1′→vi+1  ⟹  v_i\to u_i\to \overline{x_i}\to u'_{i+1}\to v_{i+1}\impliesvi​→ui​→xi​​→ui+1′​→vi+1​⟹ let τ(xi)=F\tau(x_i)=Fτ(xi​)=F
    • ∴τ\therefore\tau∴τ is valid truth assignment since xi∈V′x_i\in V'xi​∈V′ xor xi‾∈V′\overline{x_i}\in V'xi​​∈V′
  • consider each clause widget with ℓ1,ℓ2,ℓ3\ell_1,\ell_2,\ell_3ℓ1​,ℓ2​,ℓ3​
    • ∵cj⇝cj+1\because c_j\leadsto c_{j+1}∵cj​⇝cj+1​ is in ppp, thus ∃ℓi∈V′\exists\ell_i\in V'∃ℓi​∈V′
    • BWOC τ(ℓi)=F\tau(\ell_i)=Fτ(ℓi​)=F
      • ∴τ(ℓi)‾=T\therefore\overline{\tau({\ell_i})}=T∴τ(ℓi​)​=T
      • ∴ℓi‾∈V′\therefore\overline{\ell_i}\in V'∴ℓi​​∈V′ from the literal widgets
      • ∴p\therefore p∴p has cycle
    • τ(ℓi)=T\tau(\ell_i)=Tτ(ℓi​)=T
  • ∴τ\therefore\tau∴τ satisfies each clause, thus τ(φ)=T\tau(\varphi)=Tτ(φ)=T

the construction of GGG uses 4m+5n+(m+n+1)4m+5n+(m+n+1)4m+5n+(m+n+1) vertices

  • ∴\therefore∴ polytime □\square□

SUBSET-SUM Instance: ⟨S,t⟩\langle S,t\rangle⟨S,t⟩ for multiset S⊂Z+S\sub\mathbb Z^+S⊂Z+ and t∈Z+t\in\mathbb Z^+t∈Z+

Question: is there a subset S′⊆SS'\sube SS′⊆S such that sum is ttt

Proof. SS∈NPSS\in NPSS∈NP
  1. is decision
  2. for any ⟨S,t⟩∈SS\langle S,t\rangle\in SS⟨S,t⟩∈SS, the cert is a subset S′S'S′ st sum is ttt
  3. since S′⊆SS'\sube SS′⊆S, then ∣⟨S′⟩∣|\langle S'\rangle|∣⟨S′⟩∣ is polysize wrt to the input
  4. let verifier VVV be on input ⟨S,t,S′⟩\langle S,t,S'\rangle⟨S,t,S′⟩
    1. check S′⊆SS'\sube SS′⊆S
    2. let sss be sum of S′S'S′
    3. check s=ts=ts=t
    4. if all checks pass { acc } else { rej }
  5. let nnn be size of input
    • line 1 is O(n2)\mathcal O(n^2)O(n2)
    • line 2 is O(n)\mathcal O(n)O(n)
    • rest is O(1)\mathcal O(1)O(1)
    • thus VVV runs on polytime
Proof. 3S⩽pSS3S\le_p SS3S⩽p​SS

given 3CNF φ=c1∧⋯∧cm\varphi=c_1\land\cdots\land c_mφ=c1​∧⋯∧cm​ with nnn variables and mmm clauses

  • as an example, let
c1=(x1∨x2‾∨x3)c2=(x2∨x3‾∨xn)cm=(x1‾∨x3∨xn‾)c_1=(x_1\lor\overline {x_2}\lor x_3)\quad c_2=(x_2\lor\overline {x_3}\lor x_n)\quad c_m=(\overline{x_1}\lor x_3\lor\overline{x_n})c1​=(x1​∨x2​​∨x3​)c2​=(x2​∨x3​​∨xn​)cm​=(x1​​∨x3​∨xn​​)
  1. construct the following table (base 10)
x₁ x₂ .. xₙc₁ c₂ .. cₘ
x1x_1x1​1 0 .. 0 1 0 .. 0
x1‾\overline{x_1}x1​​1 0 .. 0 0 0 .. 1
x2x_2x2​0 1 .. 0 0 1 .. 0
x2‾\overline{x_2}x2​​0 1 .. 0 1 0 .. 0
⋮\vdots⋮⋮\vdots⋮⋮\vdots⋮
xnx_nxn​0 0 .. 1 0 1 .. 0
xn‾\overline{x_n}xn​​0 0 .. 1 0 0 .. 1
d1d_1d1​0 0 .. 0 1 0 .. 0
d1′d'_1d1′​0 0 .. 0 1 0 .. 0
d2d_2d2​0 0 .. 0 0 1 .. 0
d2′d'_2d2′​0 0 .. 0 0 1 .. 0
⋮\vdots⋮⋮\vdots⋮⋮\vdots⋮
dmd_mdm​0 0 .. 0 0 0 .. 1
dm′d'_mdm′​0 0 .. 0 0 0 .. 1
ttt1 1 .. 1 3 3 .. 3
  • make a table of n+mn+mn+m colums and 2(n+m)2(n+m)2(n+m) rows
  • (row xxx col xxx) for any 1⩽i,j⩽n1\le i,j\le n1⩽i,j⩽n
    • if i=ji=ji=j, then put 111 at row xix_ixi​ and col xjx_jxj​, else put 000
    • same for row xi‾\overline{x_i}xi​​
  • (row xxx col ccc) for any 1⩽i⩽n1\le i\le n1⩽i⩽n and 1⩽j⩽m1\le j\le m1⩽j⩽m
    • if xix_ixi​ in cjc_jcj​, then put 111 at row xix_ixi​ col cjc_jcj​, else put 000
    • same for now xi‾\overline {x_i}xi​​
  • (row ddd col xxx) all 000s
  • (row ddd col ccc) for any 1⩽i,j⩽m1\le i,j\le m1⩽i,j⩽m
    • if i=ji=ji=j, then put 111 at row did_idi​ and col cjc_jcj​, else put 000
    • same for row di′d'_idi′​
  • each row represents a integer where the entries are the digits
  • then construct multiset SSS as each row x1,x1‾,⋯ ,xn,xn‾,d1,d1′,⋯ ,dm,dm′x_1,\overline{x_1},\cdots,x_n,\overline {x_n},d_1,d'_1,\cdots,d_m,d'_mx1​,x1​​,⋯,xn​,xn​​,d1​,d1′​,⋯,dm​,dm′​
  • t∈Z+t\in\mathbb Z^+t∈Z+ is the last row
wts polytime construction
  • each entry is 0 or 1 or 3, thus takes polytime to make
  • each number (i.e. row) has n+mn+mn+m (i.e. polysize) digits, thus takes polytime to make
  • ∴t\therefore t∴t and each number in multiset SSS takes polytime to make
  • ∵S\because S∵S has 2n+2m2n+2m2n+2m (i.e. polysize) numbers
  • ∴S\therefore S∴S takes polytime to make
  • ∴\therefore∴ the construction takes polytime

wts satisfy   ⟹  \implies⟹ has subset assume ∃τ\exists\tau∃τ that satisfies φ\varphiφ

  • for each 1⩽i⩽n1\le i\le n1⩽i⩽n
    • if τ(xi)=T\tau(x_i)=Tτ(xi​)=T { pick row xix_ixi​ } else { pick row xi‾\overline {x_i}xi​​ }
  • ∴\therefore∴ in the picked rows,
    • each digit x1x_1x1​ to xnx_nxn​ sums to 111
    • ∵\because∵ for each cic_ici​, at least one literal is assigned to TTT by τ\tauτ
    • each digit c1c_1c1​ to cmc_mcm​ sums between 111 and 333
  • ∴\therefore∴ for each cic_ici​, can pick 222 to 000 rows among di,di′d_i,d'_idi​,di′​ to add up to 333 in each c1c_1c1​ to cmc_mcm​
  • ∴\therefore∴ we can pick rows that sums to t=1⋯13⋯3t=1\cdots13\cdots3t=1⋯13⋯3 according to the table

wts has subset   ⟹  \implies⟹ satisfy assume SSS has subset S′S'S′ that sums to ttt

  • N.B. no carry can happen when summing since base 10

  • let τ:{xi}i=1n→{T,F}\tau:\{x_i\}_{i=1}^n\to\{T,F\}τ:{xi​}i=1n​→{T,F} defined as follows

    • for each iii, if S′S'S′ has number/row xix_ixi​ then let τ(xi)=T\tau(x_i)=Tτ(xi​)=T, else let τ(xi)=F\tau(x_i)=Fτ(xi​)=F
    • ∵t\because t∵t must have 111 at every digit/col x1x_1x1​ to xnx_nxn​
    • ∴S′\therefore S'∴S′ never has both xix_ixi​ and xi‾\overline {x_i}xi​​
    • ∴τ\therefore\tau∴τ is well defined

  • consider D={di,di′}i=1mD=\{d_i,d'_i\}_{i=1}^mD={di​,di′​}i=1m​ and A=S′∖DA=S'\setminus DA=S′∖D

    • ∴A\therefore A∴A only contains numbers/row xix_ixi​ or xi‾\overline{x_i}xi​​
    • ∵\because∵ sum of numbers in DDD only has at most 222 in digits/col cic_ici​
    • ∴\therefore∴ sum of AAA must has digit at least 111 in those cols
    • ∴∀\therefore\forall∴∀ col ci∃c_i\enspace\existsci​∃ row xi∈S′x_i\in S'xi​∈S′ (or x1‾\overline{x_1}x1​​) st the digit cic_ici​ of row xix_ixi​ is 111
      • i.e. τ\tauτ assigns that literal/row in cic_ici​ to TTT

  • ∴τ\therefore\tau∴τ safisfies φ\varphiφ

(todo) JOB-SEQUENCING

Instance: let [n]={1,⋯ ,n}[n]=\{1,\cdots,n\}[n]={1,⋯,n}

  • execution times T:[n]→ZT:\mathbb [n]\to\mathbb ZT:[n]→Z
  • deadlines D:[n]→ZD:\mathbb [n]\to\mathbb ZD:[n]→Z
  • penalties P:[n]→ZP:\mathbb [n]\to\mathbb ZP:[n]→Z
  • k∈Nk\in\mathbb Nk∈N

Question: is there a permutation π:[n]→[n]\pi:[n]\to[n]π:[n]→[n] such that

∑i=1n(IπPπ)(i)⩽k\sum_{i=1}^n (I_\pi P_\pi)(i)\le ki=1∑n​(Iπ​Pπ​)(i)⩽k

where

  • Tπ=TπDπ=DπPπ=PπT_{\pi}=T\pi\quad D_\pi=D\pi\quad P_\pi=P\piTπ​=TπDπ​=DπPπ​=Pπ
  • Iπ:[n]→{0,1}I_\pi:[n]\to\{0,1\}Iπ​:[n]→{0,1} where
Iπ(i)={1∑j=1iTπ(j)>Dπ(i)0otherwiseI_\pi(i)=\begin{cases} 1&\sum_{j=1}^i T_\pi(j)>D_\pi(i)\\ 0&\textsf{otherwise} \end{cases}Iπ​(i)={10​∑j=1i​Tπ​(j)>Dπ​(i)otherwise​

In English… have nnn jobs, numbered 111 to nnn

  • job iii takes T(i)T(i)T(i) time, has deadline at D(i)D(i)D(i), has penalty P(i)P(i)P(i) if not done by D(i)D(i)D(i)
  • want to find sequence of jobs such that the total penalty is at most kkk

Proof. SS⩽pJSSS\le_p JSSS⩽p​JS let ⟨S,t⟩\langle S,t\rangle⟨S,t⟩ be an instance of SSSSSS, construct ⟨T,D,P,k⟩\langle T,D,P,k\rangle⟨T,D,P,k⟩ as instace of JSJSJS, where

  • let S={s1,⋯ ,sn}S=\{s_1,\cdots,s_n\}S={s1​,⋯,sn​}, thus n=∣S∣n=|S|n=∣S∣
  • let sπ:[n]→Ss_\pi:[n]\to Ssπ​:[n]→S where sπ(i)=sπ(i)s_\pi(i)=s_{\pi(i)}sπ​(i)=sπ(i)​
  • T(i)=P(i)=siT(i)=P(i)=s_iT(i)=P(i)=si​ and D(i)=tD(i)=tD(i)=t for any i∈[n]i\in[n]i∈[n]
  • k=(∑si)−tk=\left(\sum s_i\right)-tk=(∑si​)−t

(  ⟹  )(\implies)(⟹) assume SSS has subset XXX that sums to ttt

  • let i′=∣X∣⩽ni'=|X|\le ni′=∣X∣⩽n
  • let π:[n]→[n]\pi:[n]\to[n]π:[n]→[n] be some perm such that
    • ∀i∈[n]π(i)∈X  ⟺  i∈[i′]\forall i\in[n]\enspace \pi(i)\in X\iff i\in[i']∀i∈[n]π(i)∈X⟺i∈[i′]
  • ∴\therefore∴ for any i∈[i′]i\in[i']i∈[i′]
    ∑j=1iTπ(j)⩽∑j=1i′Tπ(j)=∑j=1i′sπ(j)=t=Dπ(i)\begin{aligned} \sum_{j=1}^iT_\pi(j)&\le\sum_{j=1}^{i'}T_\pi(j)=\sum_{j=1}^{i'}s_\pi(j)=t=D_\pi(i) \end{aligned}j=1∑i​Tπ​(j)​⩽j=1∑i′​Tπ​(j)=j=1∑i′​sπ​(j)=t=Dπ​(i)​
  • ∴Iπ(i)=0\therefore I_\pi(i)=0∴Iπ​(i)=0 for any i⩽i′i\le i'i⩽i′
∴∑i=1n(IπPπ)(i)=∑i=i′+1nPπ(i)=(∑i=1nsi)−t=k\therefore\sum_{i=1}^n (I_\pi P_\pi)(i)=\sum_{i=i'+1}^nP_\pi(i)=\left(\sum_{i=1}^ns_i\right)-t=k∴i=1∑n​(Iπ​Pπ​)(i)=i=i′+1∑n​Pπ​(i)=(i=1∑n​si​)−t=k
  • ∴⟨T,D,P,k⟩∈JS\therefore\langle T,D,P,k\rangle\in JS∴⟨T,D,P,k⟩∈JS

(  ⟸  )(\impliedby)(⟸) assume ⟨T,D,P,k⟩∈JS\langle T,D,P,k\rangle\in JS⟨T,D,P,k⟩∈JS with cert π\piπ

  • ∴k=(∑si)−t\therefore k=\left(\sum s_i\right)-t∴k=(∑si​)−t from the construction
  • let X={sπ(i):Iπ(i)=0}X=\{s_\pi(i):I_\pi(i)=0\}X={sπ​(i):Iπ​(i)=0} and i′=∣X∣i'=|X|i′=∣X∣
    • XXX is set of jobs times that have no penalty in total, i.e. meets deadline
  • let S={s0,⋯ ,sn−1}S=\{s_0,\cdots, s_{n-1}\}S={s0​,⋯,sn−1​} where si∈Xs_i\in Xsi​∈X for any 0⩽i<i′0\le i\lt i'0⩽i<i′
    • i.e. order SSS such that elements in XXX comes first
  • ∴\therefore∴ by def of IπI_\piIπ​, for any i∈[i′]i\in[i']i∈[i′] (aka meets deadline)
∑j=1iTπ(j)⩽Dπ(i)=t\sum_{j=1}^iT_\pi(j)\le D_\pi(i)=tj=1∑i​Tπ​(j)⩽Dπ​(i)=t
  • ∴\therefore∴ in the case i=i′i=i'i=i′
∑j=1i′Tπ(j)⩽t\sum_{j=1}^{i'}T_\pi(j)\le tj=1∑i′​Tπ​(j)⩽t

todo p41

TIMETABLE-SCHEDULING Instance:

  • available hours H={1,⋯ ,k}H=\{1,\cdots,k\}H={1,⋯,k}
  • teachers times T={T1,⋯ ,Tn}T=\{T_1,\cdots,T_n\}T={T1​,⋯,Tn​} where Ti⊆HT_i\sube HTi​⊆H
  • class times C={C1,⋯ ,Cm}C=\{C_1,\cdots,C_m\}C={C1​,⋯,Cm​} where Ci⊆HC_i\sube HCi​⊆H
  • required hours R=(rij)R=(r_{ij})R=(rij​)
    • rij∈Z+r_{ij}\in\mathbb Z^+rij​∈Z+ is number of hours teacher iii needs to teach class jjj

Question: is there a timetable f:[n]×[m]×H→{0,1}f:[n]\times[m]\times H\to\{0,1\}f:[n]×[m]×H→{0,1} such that

  • f(i,j,h)=1  ⟹  h∈Ti∩Cjf(i,j,h)=1\implies h\in T_i\cap C_jf(i,j,h)=1⟹h∈Ti​∩Cj​
    • assign teacher iii to class jjj at time hhh only if time hhh is available for both teacher and class
  • ∑h=1kf(i,j,h)=rij\sum_{h=1}^kf(i,j,h)=r_{ij}∑h=1k​f(i,j,h)=rij​ for any 1⩽i⩽n1\le i\le n1⩽i⩽n and 1⩽j⩽m1\le j\le m1⩽j⩽m
    • assign teacher iii exactly rijr_{ij}rij​ hours to teach class jjj
  • for any 1⩽h⩽k1\le h\le k1⩽h⩽k (i.e. any timeslot)
    • ∑i=1nf(i,j,h)⩽1\sum_{i=1}^nf(i,j,h)\le 1∑i=1n​f(i,j,h)⩽1 for any 1⩽j⩽m1\le j\le m1⩽j⩽m
      • at most one teacher is assign to class jjj
    • ∑j=1mf(i,j,h)⩽1\sum_{j=1}^mf(i,j,h)\le 1∑j=1m​f(i,j,h)⩽1 for any 1⩽i⩽n1\le i\le n1⩽i⩽n
      • at most one class is assigned to teacher iii
Proof. 3S⩽pTS3S\le_p TS3S⩽p​TS
